title: verbascum × tomentosulum
intro: Verbascum × tomentosulum, also known as the woolly mullein, is a hybrid plant species belonging to the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae). It is a biennial herb that has been used for its medicinal properties, primarily in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing stress, improving sleep, and boosting the immune system. Additionally, the plant's properties have also been used to treat skin irritations and wounds, making it a valuable herbal remedy in traditional medicine.
